channel.title: 2026-03-20 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-03-20 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Sampling-driven training of deep belief networks using a coherent Ising machine with spiking neural network
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Applied
👤 Xing-Yu Wu, Chen-Rui Fan, Yusen Wu, and Chuan Wang
Author(s): Xing-Yu Wu, Chen-Rui Fan, Yusen Wu, and Chuan Wang With the rapid advancement of generative artificial intelligence, efficiently training complex neural networks has emerged as a central challenge in deep learning. Ising machines, as specialized quantum-inspired hardware, show considerable promise for solving combinatorial optimization problems and … [Phys. Rev. Applied 25, 034067] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: High-entropy solid electrolytes discovery: a dual-stage machine learning framework bridging atomic configurations and ionic transport properties
item.description:
📚 npj Computational Materials
👤 Xiao Fu, Jing Xu, Qifan Yang, Xuhe Gong, Jingchen Lian, Liqi Wang 等9位作者
npj Computational Materials, Published online: 20 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41524-026-02041-w High-entropy solid electrolytes discovery: a dual-stage machine learning framework bridging atomic configurations and ionic transport properties

item.title: A unified ab initio theory of spin-phonon relaxation and decoherence uncovers fast dephasing in magnetic molecules
item.description:
📚 AAAS: Science Advances: Table of Contents
👤 Alessandro Lunghi
Science Advances, Volume 12, Issue 12, March 2026.

item.title: Chiral spin liquid and quantum phase transition in the triangular-lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard model
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Stefan Divic, Tomohiro Soejima (副島智大), Valentin Crépel, Michael P. Zaletel, and Andrew Millis
Author(s): Stefan Divic, Tomohiro Soejima (副島智大), Valentin Crépel, Michael P. Zaletel, and Andrew Millis Recent advances in moiré engineering motivate the study of lattice models of strongly correlated electrons subjected to substantial orbital magnetic flux. We analyze the triangular-lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard model at one-quarter flux quantum per plaquette and a density of one electron per site, wher… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L121107] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Constant Overhead Entanglement Distillation via Scrambling
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Andi Gu, Lorenzo Leone, Kenneth Goodenough, and Sumeet Khatri
Author(s): Andi Gu, Lorenzo Leone, Kenneth Goodenough, and Sumeet Khatri High-fidelity quantum entanglement enables key quantum networking capabilities such as secure communication and distributed quantum computing, but long-distance entanglement distribution is limited by noise and loss. Entanglement distillation protocols address this problem by extracting high-fidelit… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 110805] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Cr³⁺ spin dynamics under the octahedral crystal field in van der Waals antiferromagnets
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Rabindra Basnet, Subhashree Chatterjee, Paul Kigaya, Ezana Negusse, J. van Tol, and Ramesh C. Budhani
Author(s): Rabindra Basnet, Subhashree Chatterjee, Paul Kigaya, Ezana Negusse, J. van Tol, and Ramesh C. Budhani The magnetic moment in van der Waals (vdW) materials containing 3d transition metals originates from unpaired d-electron spins and their interaction with surrounding ligands. The interplay between exchange interactions and magnetic anisotropy stabilizes long-range ordering of such moments. The c… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 094441] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Design of Ferroelectric Valve: A Spin‐Valve‐Analogous Structure for Modulating Electrical Resistance
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Materials: Table of Contents
👤 Beibei Qiao, Ziyi Sun, Sheng Zhang, Tingting Yao, Yixiao Jiang, Ang Tao, Zhiqing Yang, Hengqiang Ye, Chunlin Chen
Advanced Materials, EarlyView.

item.title: Direct Raman Observation of the Quantum Metric in a Quantum Magnet
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Chao-Fan Wang, Han Ge, Jun-Yang Chen, Liusuo Wu, Xiaobin Chen, Jia-Wei Mei, and Mingyuan Huang
Author(s): Chao-Fan Wang, Han Ge, Jun-Yang Chen, Liusuo Wu, Xiaobin Chen, Jia-Wei Mei, and Mingyuan Huang The quantum geometric tensor unifies the Berry curvature (its imaginary part) and the quantum metric (its real part), yet Raman studies of chiral phonons have so far accessed only the former. We perform circularly polarized Raman spectroscopy on the quantum magnet K₂Co({math… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 116605] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Discovery of Anomalous Hall Effect in a New Noncollinear Antiferromagnetic Phase
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Materials: Table of Contents
👤 Jingyao Wang, Kewen Shi, Yuhao Jiang, Ying Sun, Sihao Deng, Jin Cui, Hongde Wang, Wenlong Cai, Daoqian Zhu, Guang Yang, Christoph Sürgers, Jiefeng Cao, Fangyuan Zhu, Yong Wang, Weisheng Zhao, Cong Wang
Advanced Materials, EarlyView.

item.title: Dynamical quantum phase transitions through the lens of mode dynamics
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Akash Mitra and Shashi C. L. Srivastava
Author(s): Akash Mitra and Shashi C. L. Srivastava We study the mode dynamics of a generic quadratic fermionic Hamiltonian under a sudden quench protocol in momentum space. Modes with zero energy at any given time, t, are referred to as dynamical critical modes. Among all zero-energy modes, spin-flip symmetry is restored in the eigenvector corresp… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 104309] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Engineering Unequal Antipolar Displacement in Ferromagnetic Layered Oxide Heterostructures
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Materials: Table of Contents
👤 Jonathan Spring, Natalya S. Fedorova, Alexander Vogel, Javier Herrero‐Martín, Evgenios Stylianidis, Pavlo Zubko, Jorge Íñiguez‐González, Marta D. Rossell, Marta Gibert
Advanced Materials, Volume 38, Issue 17, 20 March 2026.

item.title: Expansion-Driven Self-Magnetization of High-Energy-Density Plasmas
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 K. V. Lezhnin, S. R. Totorica, J. Griff-McMahon, M. V. Medvedev, H. Landsberger, A. Diallo, and W. Fox
Author(s): K. V. Lezhnin, S. R. Totorica, J. Griff-McMahon, M. V. Medvedev, H. Landsberger, A. Diallo, and W. Fox Understanding plasma self-magnetization is one of the fundamental challenges in both laboratory and astrophysical plasmas. Self-magnetization can modify plasma transport properties, altering the dynamical evolution of plasmas. Multiple high-energy-density (HED) experiments have observed the formatio… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 115101] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
